mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers immediate profit margin compression and consumer spending contraction, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative mean 20-day forward return across 100 historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the index of industrial production indicates supply-side bottlenecks that erode corporate output and profitability, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in 70 percent of quarters following an INDPRO decline, or the signal loses predictive power during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 245, persistent inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and suppressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 245, or inflation threshold shows zero predictive IC.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening that boosts consumer confidence and generates positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 5-day returns in over 50 percent of historical cases, or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in real gross domestic product directly increases corporate revenue streams and dividend payouts, creating a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no statistical relationship with GDPC1 growth rates, or the signal yields negative alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 105, manufacturing sector weakness signals impending recessionary pressures that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where INDPRO drops below 105, or the threshold signal exhibits negative Rank IC.
